=== PLAN ===
# COR24 Ecosystem Refactoring Saga

Migrate ~19 repos across 3 GitHub orgs into canonical sw-cor24-* names
under sw-embed. Strategy: rename/fork/clone+push, preserve history,
deprecate old repos, build portal site.

See docs/refactoring-plan.md for full details.

=== STEPS ===
  [ ] 001-trim-emulator [legacy]: Trim sw-cor24-emulator to its scope. Work in ~/github/sw-embed/sw-cor24-emula... <-- YOU ARE HERE
  [ ] 002-trim-assembler [legacy]: Trim sw-cor24-assembler to assembler scope. Remove emulator internals, web app, components. Keep src/assembler.rs + tests. Add path dep on ../sw-cor24-emulator. Add scripts/build.sh. Ensure tests pass.
  [ ] 003-trim-web-assembler [legacy]: Trim web-sw-cor24-assembler to web app scope. Remove CLI, rust-to-cor24, emulator/assembler source. Keep src/app.rs, components/, src/wasm.rs, src/challenge.rs, styles/, index.html, Trunk.toml. Update Cargo.toml path deps to ../sw-cor24-assembler and ../sw-cor24-emulator. Update Trunk.toml public_url. Add scripts/build-pages.sh and scripts/serve.sh. Verify trunk build works.
  [ ] 004-trim-rust [legacy]: Trim sw-cor24-rust to rust-to-cor24/ contents. Remove everything else. Restructure as top-level crate. Add path deps on ../sw-cor24-assembler and ../sw-cor24-emulator. Mark wasm2cor24 as deprecated. Add scripts/build.sh.
  [ ] 005-fork-pcode [legacy]: Fork pv24a to sw-cor24-pcode under sw-embed. Move contents into vm/ subdir. Copy pa24r into assembler/ and pl24r into linker/. Set up Rust workspace. Add scripts/build.sh. Ensure all tests pass.
  [ ] 006-fork-web-pcode [legacy]: Fork web-dv24r to web-sw-cor24-pcode under sw-embed. Update path deps to ../sw-cor24-pcode, ../sw-cor24-assembler, ../sw-cor24-emulator. Update Trunk.toml public_url. Add scripts/build-pages.sh and scripts/serve.sh.
  [ ] 007-fork-pascal [legacy]: Fork p24p to sw-cor24-pascal under sw-embed. Move contents into compiler/. Copy p24c into compiler-v2/ and pr24p into runtime/. Update paths to reference sw-cor24-pcode. Add scripts/build.sh. Ensure tests pass.
  [ ] 008-fork-web-pascal [legacy]: Fork web-p24c to web-sw-cor24-pascal under sw-embed. Update path deps. Update Trunk.toml public_url. Add scripts/build-pages.sh and scripts/serve.sh.
  [ ] 009-fork-tinyc [legacy]: Fork tc24r to sw-cor24-tinyc under sw-embed. Update path deps to ../sw-cor24-assembler and ../sw-cor24-emulator. Add scripts/build.sh.
  [ ] 010-fork-web-tinyc [legacy]: Fork web-tc24r to web-sw-cor24-tinyc under sw-embed. Update path deps and Trunk.toml. Add scripts.
  [ ] 011-fork-forth [legacy]: Fork tf24a to sw-cor24-forth under sw-embed. Update path deps. Add scripts/build.sh.
  [ ] 012-fork-web-forth [legacy]: Fork web-tf24a to web-sw-cor24-forth under sw-embed. Update path deps and Trunk.toml. Add scripts.
  [ ] 013-fork-macrolisp [legacy]: Fork tml24c to sw-cor24-macrolisp under sw-embed. Update path deps. Add scripts/build.sh.
  [ ] 014-fork-web-macrolisp [legacy]: Fork web-tml24c to web-sw-cor24-macrolisp under sw-embed. Update path deps and Trunk.toml. Add scripts.
  [ ] 015-fork-fortran [legacy]: Fork f24a to sw-cor24-fortran under sw-embed. Add scripts/build.sh.
  [ ] 016-fork-apl [legacy]: Fork a24a to sw-cor24-apl under sw-embed. Add scripts/build.sh.
  [ ] 017-deprecate-old-repos [legacy]: Add deprecation notices to all old repo READMEs. Link to new canonical repos under sw-embed.
  [ ] 018-portal-site [legacy]: Build sw-cor24-project GitHub Pages site with ISA overview, tutorials, language references, architecture diagrams, and links to all web-sw-cor24-* live demos.
  [ ] 019-verify-sweep [legacy]: Final sweep — verify all repos build/test, all demos deploy, all cross-repo links updated, all Cargo.toml package names correct, migration-status.md marked complete.

=== CURRENT STEP: 001-trim-emulator [legacy] ===
Status: pending
Description: Trim sw-cor24-emulator to its scope. Work in ~/github/sw-embed/sw-cor24-emula...

=== PROMPT ===
Trim sw-cor24-emulator to its scope. Work in ~/github/sw-embed/sw-cor24-emulator/. Remove: src/app.rs, src/challenge.rs, src/wasm.rs, components/, styles/, pages/, index.html, Trunk.toml, rust-to-cor24/. Keep: isa/, src/cpu/, src/emulator.rs, src/loader.rs, src/lib.rs, cli/, build.rs, Cargo.toml. Update Cargo.toml workspace members (remove components). Update src/lib.rs exports. Add scripts/build.sh. Ensure cargo build and cargo test pass. Commit and push.

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

content.content:
[workspace]
members = [".", "components", "cli", "isa"]
resolver = "2"

[package]
name = "cor24-emulator"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
authors = ["Michael A Wright"]
license = "MIT"
description = "COR24 C-Oriented RISC 24-bit Assembly Emulator"

[lib]
crate-type = ["cdylib", "rlib"]

[features]
default = ["standalone"]
standalone = []

[dependencies]
cor24-isa = { path = "./isa", features = ["serde"] }
components = { path = "./components" }
yew = { version = "0.21", features = ["csr"] }
wasm-bindgen = "0.2"
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1.0"
serde-wasm-bindgen = "0.6"
web-sys = { version = "0.3", features = ["console"] }
gloo = "0.11"
getrandom = { version = "0.2", features = ["js"] }
console_error_panic_hook = "0.1"
thiserror = "2.0"

[profile.release]
opt-level = "z"
lto = true

# /mw-cp — Mike Wright's Checkpoint Process

You are executing a disciplined checkpoint commit. Follow every phase in order.
Do NOT skip any phase, even if it seems clean. Report status after each phase.

---

## Phase 1 — Pre-Commit Checks

Run all of the following that apply to this project. Stop and report failures before proceeding.

1. **Format**: `cargo fmt --check` (or `rustfmt --check` on specific files)
2. **Lint**: `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
3. **Build**: `cargo build` (debug is fine; surface any compile errors)
4. **Tests**: `cargo test` — all tests must pass
5. **Unused deps** (if `cargo-machete` present): `cargo machete`
6. **Project-local checks**: run `just check` or `just pre-commit` if a justfile exists

If any check fails, STOP. Report what failed and ask the user how to proceed.

---

## Phase 2 — Documentation Updates

Review recent changes and update the following as appropriate:

1. **CHANGELOG.md / CHANGES.md** — add an entry under `[Unreleased]` or today's date
2. **STATUS.md** — update current state, what changed, what's in progress
3. **LEARNINGS.md** — capture anything notable: decisions made, gotchas, tradeoffs
4. **Inline docs** — ensure any new public API items have doc comments (`///`)
5. **README.md** — update if any user-facing behavior, config, or setup changed

If none of these files exist, note that and skip gracefully.

---

## Phase 3 — Staged Diff Review

Run `git diff --staged` (or `git diff HEAD` if nothing staged yet).

Summarize what is changing:
- Files modified, added, deleted
- High-level functional description of the change

Then stage all changes: `git add -A`

---

## Phase 4 — Commit

Compose a detailed commit message following Conventional Commits style:
```
<type>(<scope>): <short imperative summary>

<body — explain WHAT changed and WHY, not HOW>
<mention any tradeoffs, alternatives considered, or follow-ups needed>

Refs: <issue/ticket numbers if applicable>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `docs`, `test`, `chore`, `perf`, `build`

If  was provided, incorporate it as context for the message.

Execute: `git commit -m "<message>"`

---

## Phase 5 — Push

1. Check current branch: `git branch --show-current`
2. Check remote: `git remote -v`
3. Push: `git push origin <branch>`

If the push is rejected (non-fast-forward), STOP and report — do NOT force push without explicit user confirmation.

---

## Phase 6 — Checkpoint Summary

Report a brief summary:
- ✅ Checks passed
- 📝 Docs updated (list files)
- 🔖 Commit hash and message
- 🚀 Pushed to remote/branch

Flag any items that were skipped or need follow-up.
```

---
```

name: Incremental testing after each change
description: Run unit tests and integration tests after every individual change/refactoring to catch regressions immediately
type: feedback
---

Run `cargo test --workspace` after every individual change or refactoring step — not just at the end.

**Why:** Batching removals/refactors and testing only at the end leads to cascading errors that are harder to diagnose and fix. The user got burned when I deleted files in bulk, then had to chase `include_str!` references and missing methods after the fact.

**How to apply:** When trimming, refactoring, or modifying a repo:
1. Remove/change one logical group at a time (e.g., one file or one tightly-coupled set)
2. Grep for all references to what you're about to remove BEFORE removing it
3. Run `cargo test --workspace` (or equivalent) after each change
4. Only proceed to the next change when tests are green
5. For Rust repos, also use `cargo build` and `cargo clippy` as incremental gates

# Changes

## 2026-03-28

- **BREAKING**: LED active-low polarity matches hardware (0=ON, 1=OFF)
  - `IoState.leds` defaults to 1 (OFF at reset, matching Verilog `led <= 1'b1`)
  - Display code interprets bit 0 = 0 as ON
  - `EmulatorCore::is_led_on()` helper added
  - All assembler and Rust demos updated
  - Button Echo uses direct copy (no XOR), matching hardware blinky.c
- `--switch on|off` flag to set button S2 state before execution
- Improved `--dump` I/O section: LED and switch state shown separately with labels
- `--base-addr <addr>` for `--assemble` mode: labels resolve at specified base address
- `assemble_at(source, base_address)` public API on Assembler
- Fixed hello_uart.s and hello_world.s to poll UART TX busy before writes

## 2026-03-27

- `--patch <addr>=<value>` flag: write 24-bit LE values to memory after loading (repeatable)
- Binary-only mode: `--load-binary` + `--entry <addr>` without `--run` skips assembly
- `.p24` auto-detection: `--load-binary` strips 18-byte header when P24 magic detected
- `--entry` accepts numeric addresses (0x prefix, h suffix, decimal) in addition to labels
- 11 new unit tests for address parsing, patching, .p24 detection, binary loading
- Fix clippy `abs_diff` warning in isa/src/branch.rs
- Update docs/cli-tools.md with new flags, examples, and guest binary loading section
- Update [[COR24RS]] wiki page with recent changes and API docs

## 2026-03-24

- `--load-binary <file>@<addr>` flag for loading guest binaries into emulator memory

- **BREAKING**: Trim repository to emulator scope for COR24 ecosystem refactoring
  - Removed web UI: `src/app.rs`, `src/wasm.rs`, `src/c_examples.rs`, `src/rust_examples.rs`

  - Removed `.playwright-cli/`, `build.sh`, `serve.sh`, `favicon.ico`

# Changes

## 2026-03-28

- **BREAKING**: LED active-low polarity matches hardware (0=ON, 1=OFF)
  - `IoState.leds` defaults to 1 (OFF at reset, matching Verilog `led <= 1'b1`)
  - Display code interprets bit 0 = 0 as ON
  - `EmulatorCore::is_led_on()` helper added
  - All assembler and Rust demos updated
  - Button Echo uses direct copy (no XOR), matching hardware blinky.c
- `--switch on|off` flag to set button S2 state before execution
- Improved `--dump` I/O section: LED and switch state shown separately with labels
- `--base-addr <addr>` for `--assemble` mode: labels resolve at specified base address
- `assemble_at(source, base_address)` public API on Assembler
- Fixed hello_uart.s and hello_world.s to poll UART TX busy before writes

## 2026-03-27

- `--patch <addr>=<value>` flag: write 24-bit LE values to memory after loading (repeatable)
- Binary-only mode: `--load-binary` + `--entry <addr>` without `--run` skips assembly
- `.p24` auto-detection: `--load-binary` strips 18-byte header when P24 magic detected
- `--entry` accepts numeric addresses (0x prefix, h suffix, decimal) in addition to labels
- 11 new unit tests for address parsing, patching, .p24 detection, binary loading
- Fix clippy `abs_diff` warning in isa/src/branch.rs
- Update docs/cli-tools.md with new flags, examples, and guest binary loading section
- Update [[COR24RS]] wiki page with recent changes and API docs

## 2026-03-24

- `--load-binary <file>@<addr>` flag for loading guest binaries into emulator memory
- `.word` directive supports label references (forward and backward)
- UART TX busy simulation disabled in WASM mode (prevents browser freeze)
- `-h` (short help), `--help` (extended help), `-V`/`--version` flags for cor24-run
- `--terminal` mode bridging stdin/stdout to UART
- `--echo` flag for local echo in terminal mode
- `--stack-kilobytes <3|8>` for configurable EBR stack size

## 2026-03-21

- Realistic UART TX busy: 10 instruction cycles per character (was 1)
- Characters written while TX busy are dropped (not silently buffered)
- `--uart-never-ready` CLI flag: TX never clears, polling programs hang
- 3 new UART discipline tests (no-poll drops, poll works, never-ready hangs)
- Assembler rejects r3-r9 register names; only r0-r2, fp, sp, z, iv, ir
- Remove r3-r7 aliases from all docs, ISA Reference, Tutorial
- Fix `mov r6,r0` → `mov iv,r0` in echo/echo_v2 demos
- Fix UART status address mapping: MSP430 0xFF02 → COR24 0xFF0101
- TX busy poll added to all 4 Rust demos (blinky, echo, panic, uart_hello)
- Replace all "Luther Johnson" references with "MakerLisp"
- VHS tapes re-recorded with paginated display and MP4 output
